name: Tax, Compound Interest and EMIs
whatIsIt: Grown-up money maths. TAX (like GST) adds a percentage to a price: ₹100 + 18% GST = ₹118. COMPOUND INTEREST earns interest on the interest too, so money grows faster than simple interest over time. An EMI is an Equal Monthly Instalment — a loan (plus its interest) split into equal monthly payments. INFLATION means prices rise over time, so money buys a little less each year.
whyNeeded: Bills, savings, loans and shopping all use these. Understanding GST on a bill, how savings grow, what an EMI really costs, and why prices rise makes you a smart, confident money manager.
realLifeUses: GST added on a restaurant or shopping bill
Savings growing with compound interest at a bank
A phone or bike bought on EMIs
Why the same item costs more each year (inflation)

vocabulary.meaning: A percentage added to a price (e.g. 18% GST).
Interest earned on both the principal AND the interest already added.
Equal Monthly Instalment — a loan repaid in equal monthly amounts.

story.title: The Bill with GST
story.text: Naina's food costs ₹200, and the bill adds 5% GST. She works out 5% of 200 = ₹10, so the total is 200 + 10 = ₹210. 'Tax is a percentage ON TOP,' she says. Her uncle adds, 'and if you saved ₹200 at compound interest, next year you'd earn interest on the interest too — money grows on money.'
story.extractedProblem: Food costs ₹200 with 5% GST. What is the total bill?

visual.caption: Tax adds a percentage on top of the price: ₹200 + (5% of 200 = ₹10) = ₹210.

teachingGallery.title: Add tax, grow money, split loans
teachingGallery.note: Tax adds %; compound grows on the new total; EMI splits the total.

examples.caption: Year 2 earns interest on the interest too.
EMI = total (with interest) ÷ number of months.

standardMethod.summary: Tax: add the % of the price. Compound interest: apply interest each period on the running total. EMI: divide the total owed by the number of months.
steps: Tax total = price + (tax% of price). GST 18% on ₹100 → 100 + 18 = ₹118.
Simple interest is on the original only; COMPOUND adds interest to the total, so the next interest is bigger.
EMI ≈ total amount to repay (loan + interest) ÷ number of months.
Inflation: prices rise by a % each year, so plan for costs to grow.

workedExamples.problem: A ₹500 item has 10% GST. What is the total price?
₹1000 at 10% per year, compounded. What is the amount after 2 years?

commonMistakes.mistake: Giving the tax amount as the total instead of adding it to the price.
commonMistakes.fix: Tax is ON TOP: total = price + tax. ₹500 with ₹50 tax = ₹550, not ₹50.

commonMistakes.mistake: Treating compound interest like simple interest (same interest every year).
commonMistakes.fix: Compound interest is charged on the NEW, bigger total each period — so it grows faster than simple interest.

easy.q: 10% GST on ₹200 is how many rupees of tax?

medium.q: A ₹400 item has 5% GST. Total price (₹)?
₹12000 loan repaid over 12 equal months. EMI (₹)?

challenge.q: A ₹500 item has ₹50 tax. Ravi says the price is ₹50. What's the fix?

questions.q: 18% GST on ₹100 is how much tax (₹)?
Compared with simple interest, compound interest…

teachBackPrompt: Look at a real bill with GST at home. Show a grown-up how the tax is a percentage added on top, and explain the difference between simple and compound interest — why saving with compound interest grows money faster over the years.

revisionCard.summary: Tax/GST: total = price + (tax% of price). Compound interest: interest on the growing total each period (grows faster than simple). EMI = total owed (loan + interest) ÷ number of months. Inflation: prices rise a % each year, so money buys less over time.

meta.curriculum: CBSE/NCERT Class 8 (tax/GST, compound interest, EMI, inflation)
